questions.question: Why is first-touch attribution better than last-touch for solo founders making channel investment decisions?

options.text: It tells you which channel is best at bringing new leads in
It gives credit to all touchpoints

questions.explanation: First-touch attribution reveals which channels are best at acquisition — the decision solo founders need to make when allocating limited time and budget.

questions.question: A lead discovers you via a blog post, engages on LinkedIn for 2 months, then closes after an email. Using first-touch attribution, which channel gets credit?
questions.explanation: First-touch attribution credits the channel that first brought the lead into your world — in this case, the blog post (Content/Inbound).

questions.question: What is 'dark social' in the context of attribution?

options.text: Traffic from private channels you can't track (Slack, text, DMs)

questions.explanation: Dark social refers to conversions that happen through private, untrackable channels like Slack messages, texts, or private DMs — often showing up as 'Direct' traffic.

questions.question: You spend 20 hours/month on LinkedIn (free tool) and generate 1 deal worth $2,000. Your hourly rate is $100. What's your channel ROI?
questions.explanation: Total cost = $0 (tool) + (20 hours × $100/hr) = $2,000. Revenue = $2,000. ROI = $2,000 / $2,000 = 1:1 (breakeven). Your time is a cost.

questions.question: Which metric is most important when deciding which channel to scale?
questions.explanation: Revenue per lead reveals which channel produces the highest-quality leads. This is the key metric for scaling decisions.

questions.question: Why might content marketing look weak in Month 1-3 but strong in Month 6+ of attribution analysis?

options.text: Content has a 3-6 month sales cycle lag

questions.explanation: Content-sourced leads often have longer sales cycles (3-6 months). Cohort analysis reveals this pattern and prevents you from killing content too early.

questions.question: What should you do if a channel has high volume but low revenue per lead?

options.text: Optimize targeting and messaging to improve conversion
Ignore it and focus on other channels

questions.explanation: High volume + low conversion = optimization opportunity. Improve targeting, messaging, or qualification to unlock the volume's potential.

questions.question: How often should solo founders review attribution data?
questions.explanation: Weekly reviews (5 minutes) keep you aware of trends. Monthly deep dives (30 minutes) drive channel allocation decisions.
